attributes.stem:
A 28-year-old woman attends her General Practice clinic reporting a seven-week history of amenorrhoea and nausea. Urine b-hCG in the clinic is positive and a bedside ultrasound confirms an intrauterine pregnancy. The patient is G4P3, has no significant medical history and takes no regular medications.

The patient decides that she does not want to continue with the pregnancy, and requests a referral to a family planning clinic which the General Practitioner provides. At the clinic one week later, ultrasound confirms a live intrauterine pregnancy. The patient is provided with medication for a medical termination of pregnancy.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most appropriate choice for medical termination of pregnancy for this patient?
attributes.explanation:
This patient is seeking a termination of pregnancy. The gestation of a pregnancy is vital to deciding what option is most appropriate management. From her history, she has an estimated gestation of 8/40 based on LMP. At eight weeks, there are multiple options appropriate for termination of pregnancy (TOP), including medical and surgical TOP.

Medical TOP at <63 days can be done through outpatient misoprostol and mifepristone therapy. The patient will take the medications as prescribed, and ideally pass the products of conception (POC) at home. Success of MTOP is estimated at 95.16%. While complications are rare, the most common is retained clots. Risk of ongoing pregnancy is very low, however patients should have a review within one week to confirm passing of POC - if there is evidence of an ongoing pregnancy, a repeat misoprostol dose or surgical TOP is needed. It is important to safety net a patient undergoing an outpatient medical termination of pregnancy for risk factors for when to represent (ie. excessive bleeding, signs of infection, failure of treatment).

Misoprostol and mifepristone is a dual therapy used for medical TOP. Misoprostol is a prostaglandin analogue and is often used for medical management of retained products and is appropriate in patients who do not wish to continue with their pregnancy, or who have been diagnosed with a missed miscarriage. Mifepristone is an anti-progesterone agent which helps prime the uterus, and should be given orally 48 hours prior to misoprostol admission, up to 63 days gestation.

Suction dilation and curettage is a surgical option for TOP up to 14 weeks gestation. Dilation and curettage is a surgical procedure performed under general anaesthetic where the cervix is dilated, and the endometrial lining and associated tissue/POC are scraped away from the uterus and removed. It is also indicated in those who have had a miscarriage and are not eligible for (or not responded to) expectant or medical management of a miscarriage, or who choose to not trial medical management first.

Inpatient misoprostol and mifepristone therapy is appropriate for TOP in a pregnancy greater than 63 days (9+0/40) gestation, due to the size of the fetus and presence of a substantiated placenta.

Misoprostol single therapy is not best practise for this patient.

Methotrexate is a cytotoxic drug which interrupts the function of folic acid to stop cell division and proliferation. It is used in those with a confirmed ectopic pregnancy less than 5cm diameter who are hemodynamically stable, with a serum b-hCG less than 5000mlU/ml. Methotrexate does not have any role in medical TOP.
learningPoints: Medical termination of pregnancy less than 63 days gestation can be managed with mifepristone and misoprostol dual therapy as an outpatient.
references: Termination of Pregnancy (Clinical guideline [ng140]). In: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. [Online]. 2019; updated 2019 Apr; cited 2021 Feb 8. Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng140/documents/draft-guideline

The use of mifepristone for medical abortion. In: The Royal Australian and New Zealand College of Obstetrics and Gynaecology, Clinical Practice Guideline. [Online]. 2007; updated 2019 Mar; cited 2021 Feb. Available at: https://ranzcog.edu.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/The-use-of-misoprostol-in-obstetrics-and-gynaecology.pdf
Mazza D, Burton G, Wilson S, Boulton E, Fairweather J, Black K. Medical abortion. Aust J Gen Pract. 2020;49:324-30. Available at: https://www1.racgp.org.au/ajgp/2020/june/medical-abortion
